did.unittitle: Freeling correspondence

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Letters to Freeling, mainly concerning his work for the postal service. Some letters contain a draft of Freeling's reply.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Bequest of James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Freeling Correspondence is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Freeling Correspondence. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

bioghist.head: SIR FRANCIS FREELING, 1764-1836
bioghist.p: Sir Francis Freeling was born in Radcliffe Parish, Bristol, on August 25, 1764. His first employment was with the Bristol Post Office, but when the new Palmer system of mail coaches was established in 1785, Freeling was appointed to assist Palmer. In 1787 he joined the General Post Office in London, where he served for almost half a century, rising finally to the office of Sole Secretary.

Freeling's management of the postal service was widely praised, and in 1828 a baronetcy was conferred on him for his public service. He was also a book collector and antiquarian, a Fellow of the Society of Antiquaries and a founding member of the Roxburghe Club. He died at home in Bryanston Square, London, in 1836, and was succeeded in the baronetcy by his eldest son, Sir George Henry Freeling, an Oxford alumnus and Commissioner of Customs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Freeling Correspondence consists almost entirely of business letters addressed to Sir Francis Freeling as an employee of the Post Office. The letters are mostly by members of the peerage, suggesting that they may have been selected from Freeling's files and saved for their autograph value. Authors include the Earl of Arran, the Duke of Beaufort, the Duke of Buckingham and Chandos, Lord Elcho, and the Earl of Chichester. Subjects include franking privileges; mail routes and mail delivery times; delayed or lost mail; recommendations for employment; and requests for favors. Some of the letters are docketed with summaries of Freeling's replies.

Among the more substantive items are a letter from the Earl of Cardigan concerning a large theft by a servant; complaints by Baron Dulcie of repeatedly delayed mail; and an MP's complaint that his franks are not being honored. An 1809 letter from Edward Cooke, Undersecretary of State for War, explains the Army's position on mail privileges for officers "it is a matter of much accommodation to the officer and the loss to revenue is but casual." And a letter from Freeling himself to the Postmasters-General in 1810 takes issue with an anonymous complaint about working conditions in the Post Office, noting that "the Income of the Letter Carriers, considering the order in Society from which they are selected and the duty they have to perform, is sufficiently ample."

did.unittitle: Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence is arranged alphabetically by author.

did.unittitle: ARBUTHNOT, CHARLES, 1767-1850
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Treasury Chambers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arbuthnot, citing his franking privileges, complains letters have returned to him.

did.unittitle: ARRAN, ARTHUR SAUNDERS, EARL OF, 1761-1837
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Informs Freeling of his support for a Mr. Bosauquet's election to an unnamed club or position.

did.unittitle: AUCKLAND, WILLIAM EDEN, LORD, 1744-1814
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Eden Farm
ALS to Francis Freeling; Eden Farm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Holiday greetings to the Freelings from the Aucklands, who plan to "settle" in town from 18 Jan to 10 May.

did.unittitle: BARRY, JOHN M.
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Treasury Chambers, Dublin Castle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Barry requests Freeling to hold some postal packets for him. Freeling's reply (copy) is included on the letter.

did.unittitle: BEAUFORT, HENRY CHARLES SOMERSET, DUKE OF, 1766-1835
ALS to Francis Freeling; Grosvenor Square

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning Beaufort's mother's suggestion to leave a letter bag at her gate at Stoke near Bristol.

did.unittitle: BUCKINGHAM AND CHANDOS, RICHARD TEMPLE

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: [NUGENT BRYDGES CHANDOS GRENVILLE, DUKE OF, 1776-1839]

did.unittitle: ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Pall Mall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chandos requests delivery of a letter of the "utmost importance."

did.unittitle: CARDIGAN, ROBERT BRUDENELL, EARL OF, 1769-1837
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Portman Square

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: States that Wm. Upton has absconded with a very large sum of money.

did.unittitle: CARYSFORT, JOHN JOSHUA PROBY, EARL OF, 1751-1828
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Archlow [Ireland]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Carysfort asks Freeling to frank, "on a literary matter, a pretty bulky packed [sent] to Mr. George Ellis, at Sunning Hill or Clarenont."

did.unittitle: CAWDOR, JOHN FREDERICK, EARL OF, 1790-1860
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Stackpole Court

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cawdor inquires about the route of mail between London and Cork, Ireland. Note on Docket states Dublin.

did.unittitle: CHAMPION DE CRESPIGNY, WILLIAM, SIR, 1765-1829
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Southampton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: De Crespigny seeks Freeling's aid.

did.unittitle: CHARTERIS, FRANCIS, (LORD ELCHO), 1749-1808
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Grosvenor Street
CHESTERFIELD, HENRIETTA (THYNNE) STANHOPE,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: [COUNTESS OF, 1762-1813]

did.unittitle: ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Chesterfield House

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lady Chesterfield lobbies for the appointment of a Mr. Dunn as letter carrier.

did.unittitle: CHICHESTER, MARY HENRIETTA JULIANA (GODOLPHIN)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: [PELHAM, COUNTESS OF, 1776-1862]

did.unittitle: 2 ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Stanmer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning the 5th birthday celebration of her eldest son Henry (b. 25 Aug 1804).

did.unittitle: ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Stanmer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning her husband Lord Chichester's cold which has kept him three days abed.

did.unittitle: CIPRIANI, [PHILIP]
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Treasury

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cipriani recommends a Mr. Knowles for employment. Copy of Freeling's response on reverse side.

did.unittitle: CLAYTON, WILLIAM ROBERT, SIR, 1786-1866
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Boulogne-sur-Mer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clayton warns Freeling that a letter with Freeling's frank mailed to Calais had produced a charge of postage due there and Freeling is admonished not to allow such a precedent to be set.

did.unittitle: COOKE, EDWARD, 1755-1820
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Downing Street

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cooke, Undersecretary of State for War and colonies, concerning abuses of the mail by the army and foreign services.

did.unittitle: CURREY, EDMUND
2 ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Bath and Bankhouse

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Currey writes on behalf of the Duke of Gloucester.

did.unittitle: DERBY, EDWARD SMITH STANLEY, EARL OF, 1752-1834
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Grosvenor Square

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Derby thanks Freeling for his attention in securing a Post Mastership for an individual.

did.unittitle: DULCIE, FRANCIS REYNOLDS-MORETON, BARON, 1739-1808
LS to Sir Francis Freeling; Tortworth

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dulcie complains about postal service delays.

did.unittitle: FREELING, FRANCIS, SIR, 1764-1836
Letter (copy) to Charles Arbuthnot, General Post Office
Letter (copy) to Mr. Hindell at Truro, [n.p.]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Freeling states: "... an Official Man cannot indulge those feelings and inclinations which would at once influence him as a Private Individual .

did.unittitle: Letter to [the Earls of Chichester and Sandwich], General Post Office

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chichester and Sandwich, serving in 1810 as Postmasters-General. Freeling's letter concerns an anonymous letter of complaint, apparently from a letter carrier. Docketed response from Chichester urges Freeling to "attempt ... to discover the writer of this letter."

did.unittitle: GARDINER, ROBERT WILLIAM, SIR, 1781-1864
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Cassell
GREVILLE, CHARLES FRANCIS, 1749-1809
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Stackpole Court
GREVILLE, ROBERT FULKE, 1751-1824
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Mayfair
HARTWELL, FRANCIS JOHN, SIR, 1757-1831
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Dorchester
HOBHOUSE, HENRY, 1776-1854
ALS to Francis Freeling; Whitehall
ALS to Lt. Col. William Leader Maberly, Wincanton
JONES, WILLIAM
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Cassel
MALMESBURY, JAMES HARRIS, EARL OF, 1746-1820
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Huru Court, Christchurch
PELHAM, THOMAS, EARL OF CHICHESTER, 1756-1826
3 ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Eridge Castle and Stanmer
POLE, L. S.
ALS to Sir Francis Freeling; Oxford Street
